summary: Accept the conventional comment-create, issue-read, and document-read command phrases agents repeatedly try while retaining the compact canonical command surface.
reason: Routine agent invocations repeatedly lose a turn to conventional but unsupported verbs. Hidden aliases should share the canonical handlers so body files, media, comma-separated reads, data-source selection, and output flags cannot drift.
validated_outcome: Regression tests prove comments create matches comments add for literal body files and repeated media, issue get/view/show preserve ordered comma reads and global flags, document get/view preserve positional reads, which routes conventional phrasing to canonical commands, and unsupported subcommands identify the invalid token.
